title: Desmos pannosus
intro: Desmos pannosus, also known as Kukui, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Hawaii, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fragrant, oily seeds. The health benefits of Desmos pannosus include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant agent, making it useful in treating various conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include desmosine, a lignan that has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Desmos pannosus is often used to make herbal preparations, such as infused oils, salves, and teas, which are used to treat skin conditions, promote wound healing, and soothe respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Desmos pannosus is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Australia, specifically the eastern and southern regions. The plant is a shrub or a small tree that grows up to 6 meters in height. Desmos pannosus belongs to the genus Desmos, which consists of several species of flowering plants. It is also known to be part of the order Asterales, which includes various flowering plant families.
